q: What is Centauri
t: what is overview about definition introduction bi-temporal causal event database
a: Centauri is an open-source **bi-temporal, causal, AI-first event database**. It keeps every fact on two clocks — when it was true and when you learned it — so you can query the past, replay what you believed, trace why things changed, prove history wasn't tampered with, and give AI agents a memory they can reason over.

q: What problems does Centauri solve
t: problems solve use cases pain points why for
a: Four questions ordinary databases can't answer: what was true at a past moment, what we believed then, why something changed, and whether the record was tampered with. It also flags changes that were sent but never landed, and gives agents point-in-time, provenance-aware memory.

q: What kind or type of database is Centauri
t: what kind type category relational document key value keyvalue ledger nosql sql rdbms oltp olap store
a: An **event database**: the atom is an immutable, bi-temporal, causal fact — not a row, document, or key-value pair. Its facets and flexible values can model relational or document-like data, and its tamper-evident hash chain gives ledger-like integrity (without being a blockchain). It's the system of record for change, beside your operational store.

q: Is Centauri memory for AI agents
t: memory ai agent agents context recall remember rag long term knowledge
a: Yes — that's the core positioning. Agents need what humans need to decide well: a record of what happened, where it came from, how much to trust it, and why it changed. Centauri stores all of that and lets agents query it natively over MCP, with no token cost to remember.

q: Does Centauri scale and how much data can it hold
t: scale scaling ram memory sharding cluster volume capacity big data petabyte limits how much
a: It's a single-node engine with read replicas; indexes live in memory, so the working set must fit in RAM. It's the system of record for facts that matter (prices, decisions, state, audit) — a flight recorder beside your operational stores, not a petabyte warehouse. Shard by tenant or region for large estates.

q: Is Centauri free and what is the license
t: free license cost open source pricing money commercial paid
a: Yes — **free and open source** under a PostgreSQL-style permissive license. No paid tiers, no telemetry.

q: Can Centauri do compute and analytics
t: compute computation analytics calculate aggregate aggregation cepl procedure topology math analysis processing
a: Yes. It runs aggregations (`COUNT`/`SUM`/`AVG`/`GROUP BY`/`HAVING`), topology (persistent homology, sheaf consistency), BM25 search, vector similarity, and CePL stored procedures — all in pure Go over the in-memory working set. It's analytical, not a distributed compute grid.

q: What is bi-temporal valid and transaction time
t: bitemporal bi-temporal two clocks valid transaction time as of as known at time travel history audit replay
a: Two independent clocks on every fact: **valid time** (when it was true) and **transaction time** (when Centauri learned it). `AS OF` travels valid time; `AS KNOWN AT` travels transaction time. Together they replay exactly what you believed at any past moment.

q: What is a facet
t: facet facets register shelf source storecentral system view reality per system
a: A facet is one system's view of a subject — register, shelf, storecentral, source, etc. The same item can hold different facet values, which is how `CONSISTENCY` and `DISAGREE` spot systems that don't match.

q: What is the data model an event a fact a subject
t: data model event fact subject value provenance confidence atom structure
a: The atom is an **Event**: a fact about a subject, on a facet, with a value, two timestamps, provenance, confidence, and causal links. Events are immutable; only supersession markers are ever added.

q: Is data immutable does it support delete or update
t: immutable delete update modify edit retire supersede append remove change overwrite
a: There is no destructive update or delete. An update is a new superseding fact; a delete is `RETIRE` (the current fact stops applying, history kept). Nothing is ever erased — that's the point.

q: What is CeQL the query language
t: ceql query language syntax sql grammar how to query
a: CeQL is Centauri's query language where time, cause, trust, shape, and meaning are syntax. One semantics, three surfaces: humans write text, agents emit the JSON AST, legacy speaks REST.

q: How do I write or insert or update data
t: write insert add put update record create save data ingest load
a: Writing is one act: `PUT subject SET field=value, ...`. Insert and update are the same — a new fact supersedes the old, and the old stays in history. Bulk-load with the SDK's `db.pump('data.csv')`.

q: Does it support aggregations and group by
t: aggregate aggregation group by count sum avg having analytics rollup statistics
a: Yes: `FACTS facet, COUNT(*), AVG(price_cents) OF item:* GROUP BY facet HAVING COUNT(*) > 5`. Aggregations respect time travel, so you can roll up as of any past moment.

q: Does Centauri support joins
t: join joins relational foreign key relationship link
a: Not SQL-style table joins. Relationships are modeled with subjects/facets and first-class causal links (`WHY`/`EFFECTS`), and `CONTEXT FOR` assembles everything about a subject in one call.

q: Why did it change causality and lineage
t: why cause causal lineage effects root cause reason explain trace links
a: Causes are first-class links. `WHY` walks the chain back to the root; `EFFECTS` walks it forward. No war-room archaeology to explain a value.

q: Does Centauri do full-text search BM25
t: search full text bm25 keyword find lookup ranked relevance inverted index
a: Yes — native ranked full-text search: `SEARCH 'late markdown' OF item:*` scores with **BM25** in pure Go, no inverted index to maintain, and is bi-temporal. It also blends with vectors for hybrid retrieval.

q: Hybrid keyword and vector search for RAG
t: hybrid vector semantic embedding similar rag retrieval cosine knn
a: `SEARCH '...' SIMILAR TO <event> ALPHA 0.5` blends BM25 keyword scores with embedding similarity — the recall of semantics with the precision of keywords. Ideal for grounding RAG.

q: Is Centauri a vector database
t: vector database vectordb pinecone weaviate qdrant chroma milvus pgvector embedding store ann nearest neighbor semantic
a: Not a **dedicated** vector database, but it has vector search built in: embeddings as supersedable facts, `SIMILAR TO` for cosine search, and hybrid keyword+vector. For billion-scale pure ANN a dedicated vector DB still wins; Centauri shines when vectors live next to bi-temporal, causal facts.

q: Is Centauri a graph database
t: graph database neo4j relationships edges nodes cypher gremlin property graph network
a: Not a general property-graph database, but causality is first-class: causal links are edges, and `WHY`/`EFFECTS` walk them. It's a lineage/causal graph over events, not a Neo4j-style analytics engine.

q: Is Centauri a time-series database
t: time series timeseries tsdb influxdb prometheus timescale metrics telemetry sensor high frequency
a: It's temporal (bi-temporal) and detects periodicity and drift — but not a metrics TSDB for high-frequency telemetry. Use a TSDB for the firehose; use Centauri for facts and decisions that need time-travel, cause, and trust.

q: Built-in topology shape clusters loops voids drift periodicity
t: topology shape homology cluster loop void periodic seasonal drift consistency cycles tda persistent
a: Topology operators see the shape of data: `SHAPE` (persistent homology — clusters, loops, voids, periodicity), `CONSISTENCY` (sheaf agreement and the outlier), `CYCLES` (causal-graph integrity), `DRIFT` (distribution change). Pure Go, zero deps.

q: Is Centauri built for AI agents and MCP
t: ai agent agents mcp llm model context protocol gateway assistant copilot tools
a: Yes — a native **MCP server** lets agents speak CeQL directly as typed tool calls. Plain English compiles to queries with no tokens, `AS KNOWN AT` gives leak-free training features, and the Genesis Engine builds a database from a description.

q: Can I ask in plain English natural language
t: natural language english plain translate nl no tokens deterministic ask query
a: Yes — type a question in English and Centauri compiles it to CeQL with **deterministic rules**: no tokens, no API key, no hallucinated SQL.

q: Leak-free training features for ML and RAG
t: ml machine learning training features leakage point in time skew model rag data science
a: `AS KNOWN AT` reconstructs features exactly as they were known at prediction time — eliminating training-serving skew and label leakage. The clean way to build historical training sets and reproducible RAG context.

q: What is the Genesis engine
t: genesis build generate scaffold interview blueprint create describe schema
a: Describe a domain in plain language; the **Genesis Engine** interviews you, then builds schemas, procedures, watches, and starter data — and stores the conversation as facts inside the database it created.

q: What is CePL stored procedures
t: cepl stored procedure function plsql logic run trace
a: **CePL** is near-English stored procedures, kept as versioned facts with a step-by-step execution trace — PL/SQL rebuilt for auditability. Call them with `RUN <proc> WITH ...`.

q: Does it have schemas and validation
t: schema schemas validation define types required constraints versioned
a: Yes — define versioned, append-only schemas with `DEFINE SCHEMA`; writes that break a schema are rejected. Schemas evolve without migrations; new versions supersede old ones.

q: Can I embed the assistant or a live agent on my website
t: embed widget website live agent chatbot support bot script iframe self host zero token docs assistant
a: Yes. This assistant is a self-contained, **zero-token** BM25 widget that indexes your page content — a free docs/support bot with no LLM bill. For answers that learn over time, put a running Centauri behind it: `ASK` searches your `kb:*` facts and logs gaps as `kb_gap:*` for an agent to fill.

q: How does the assistant learn new answers
t: learn learning self learning kb gap teach train improve knowledge base ask grow
a: `ASK '...'` answers from `kb:*` facts via BM25; on a miss it records a `kb_gap:*` fact. An AI agent reads the gaps over MCP, answers them, and appends a new `kb:*` fact — so the same question is answered from the database forever after. Centauri is the gateway and the memory.

q: Tamper-evident hash chain and verify
t: tamper verify hash chain integrity audit sha256 trust proof immutable
a: Every committed fact is sealed into a **SHA-256 chain**. `centauri verify` recomputes it byte-for-byte and points at the exact record where anything changed, and everything downstream of it.

q: Changes that never landed pending wedge disagree
t: pending wedge disagree never activated landed sync silent failure distributed config
a: Centauri models each system as a facet and tracks whether a change was distributed but never activated. `PENDING pdt OLDER THAN 21 DAYS` finds silent failures; `DISAGREE ON price_cents` finds systems that disagree now.

q: How do I install and download Centauri
t: install download setup get started run binary windows mac macos linux installer curl
a: From the Download section: a Windows installer, or one line on macOS/Linux (`curl ... | sh`). Then `centauri desktop` opens the dashboard in your browser.

q: What are the system requirements
t: requirements hardware os ram cpu prerequisites needs minimum platform
a: Windows 10/11, macOS, or Linux (64-bit); enough RAM to hold your working set (indexes live in memory); no other dependencies — one static binary.

q: How do I get started quickly
t: quickstart getting started first steps tutorial begin demo seed
a: Install, then `centauri seed` (demo data), `centauri desktop` (opens the dashboard), then run `FACTS OF item:100001/store:4001`.

q: What is desktop mode and the dashboard
t: desktop dashboard browser ui run start gui interface workbench
a: `centauri desktop` keeps data in your user profile and opens a zero-install dashboard — query workbench, visual builder, live feed, and the interactive CeQL textbook, all embedded in the binary.

q: Is it ACID and does it support transactions
t: acid transaction transactions consistency atomicity isolation oltp commit rollback
a: Writes are atomic per append and durable (write-then-apply), and a single node is strongly consistent. It is not a multi-statement OLTP transaction engine with rollback — use your operational RDBMS for that.

q: Is my data durable and crash-safe
t: durability crash recovery persistence wal write ahead log restart safe fsync
a: Yes — every fact is written to an append-only log before memory changes, so a crash replays cleanly on restart, and the SHA-256 chain proves the log is intact.

q: How does it handle concurrency and many writers
t: concurrency concurrent writers parallel locking mvcc threads
a: A single durable append log serializes writes; reads are concurrent and lock-light against the in-memory indexes. It's optimized for many readers and a steady write stream, not high-contention multi-master writes.

q: How do authentication and access control work
t: security auth authentication token access control permission read only api key bearer
a: The HTTP API is gated by a bearer token, with an optional second **read-only** token; followers can run read-only. Put it behind TLS.

q: Does it encrypt data at rest and in transit
t: encryption encrypt at rest tls in transit secure ssl https
a: In transit: run it behind HTTPS (Render provides it). At rest: encryption with crypto-erasure is on the roadmap; today use disk/volume encryption.

q: Is it good for audit and compliance SOX HIPAA
t: compliance audit sox hipaa regulatory governance trail evidence chain of custody
a: Its bi-temporal, never-erased, tamper-evident log is built for audit: prove what was true, what you knew, and that nothing was altered — exactly what SOX/HIPAA-style reviews ask for.

q: GDPR right to be forgotten with immutable history
t: gdpr erasure right to be forgotten delete privacy compliance forget personal data retention ccpa
a: Deletion is two-layered: `RETIRE` stops a fact from applying (history kept), and crypto-erasure (roadmap) destroys the key for a subject's encrypted payloads — unrecoverable while preserving the tamper-evident chain.

q: Where does my data live and is there telemetry
t: privacy telemetry data location phone home tracking local sovereignty residency
a: Your data stays wherever you run Centauri — your machine or your cloud. No telemetry, no phone-home.

q: Is there a REST API or HTTP interface
t: rest api http endpoint json curl integrate query web
a: Yes — `POST /v1/query` runs CeQL or a JSON AST; also `/v1/context`, `/v1/asof`, `/v1/pending`, `/v1/watch` (SSE). Any language that makes HTTP calls can use Centauri.

q: What languages and clients are supported
t: language client driver python javascript java go sdk binding library
a: A zero-dependency Python SDK ships today; everything else uses the HTTP API (any language), and agents use the MCP server. CeQL's JSON AST makes typed clients trivial.

q: How do I import or export data
t: import export csv jsonl bulk load migrate pump dump backfill
a: Bulk-load with the SDK's `db.pump('data.csv')` or the HTTP ingest endpoint; export with `centauri export -q 'FACTS OF *' -format csv` (or jsonl).

q: Does it support replication
t: replication replica follow ship read replica high availability ha failover
a: Yes — readable replicas via log shipping with `centauri follow`. Replicas can serve read-only traffic; the primary owns writes.

q: How do I back up and restore
t: backup restore snapshot recover copy disaster recovery
a: `centauri backup` copies the committed log and verifies the hash chain matches; restore by pointing at the backup file. `centauri follow` keeps a live readable replica.

q: Does it support multitenancy and namespaces
t: multitenant tenant namespace environments isolation per customer saas
a: Yes — many isolated databases per server, snapshot cloning, namespaces for shared-schema tenancy (a subject's first segment is its namespace), and two-tier access tokens.

q: Streaming and standing queries WATCH
t: watch stream subscribe live realtime trigger standing query feed notify events sse
a: `WATCH` is a standing query — stream every new fact (or a filtered subset) as it commits. The dashboard's live feed and SDK `db.watch()` ride the same channel.

q: How do I monitor it health and stats
t: monitoring health stats metrics observability counters status uptime
a: `STATS` (or `/v1/stats`) returns live counters — events, subjects, open facts, pending wedges, links — and `centauri verify` checks integrity. The dashboard shows it live.

q: How fast is it performance and latency
t: performance fast speed latency throughput benchmark memory in-memory
a: Reads serve from in-memory indexes (sub-millisecond point queries); writes are append-then-apply with a durable log. Built for correctness and rich queries over a working set that fits in RAM, not petabyte OLAP.

q: Is Centauri production ready and stable
t: production ready stable stability maturity reliable battle tested beta enterprise
a: Centauri is young (v0.3.0). Use it as the system of record for facts, audit, and lineage alongside your proven operational store — not yet the sole database for a critical transactional workload. It has a deterministic test suite, a tamper-evident log, and chain-verified backups.

q: Deployment Docker Render cloud and Kubernetes
t: deploy deployment docker render cloud host server vps https kubernetes container production
a: One-click Deploy to Render (HTTPS + token in your own account), or any Docker host. See the deployment guide in the repo.

q: Can it run offline or on a laptop
t: offline laptop local air gapped no internet portable single binary edge
a: Yes — one self-contained binary with no network dependencies, so it runs offline on a laptop or in an air-gapped environment.

q: How is Centauri different from PostgreSQL
t: postgresql postgres compare versus difference replace relational sql
a: It isn't a Postgres replacement — it's the layer Postgres lacks: bi-temporal history, causal lineage, tamper-evidence, topology, native search, and an agent interface. Run it beside Postgres as the record of what happened, when, why, and how much to trust it.

q: How does it compare to Oracle DB2 SQL Server MongoDB
t: compare comparison versus oracle db2 sqlserver sql server mongodb mongo mysql cassandra snowflake alternative replace traditional rdbms
a: Centauri complements traditional databases (Oracle, DB2, SQL Server, MongoDB) rather than replacing them. They stay your high-volume operational store; Centauri sits beside them as the bi-temporal, causal, tamper-evident record of change.

q: How does it compare to Datomic or EventStoreDB
t: datomic eventstore eventstoredb immutable temporal event sourcing compare versus
a: Like Datomic it's immutable and time-aware, like EventStoreDB it's an event log — but Centauri adds the second clock (bi-temporal), causal links, tamper-evident hashing, topology, native search, and an MCP interface, in one zero-dependency binary.

q: How is it different from Kafka event streaming
t: kafka streaming event bus pulsar queue broker pub sub stream
a: Kafka is a streaming bus that moves events; Centauri is the database that stores and queries them bi-temporally, causally, and by shape. Complementary — stream with Kafka, remember with Centauri (`WATCH` streams new facts out).

q: Do I still need Elasticsearch
t: elasticsearch opensearch elastic lucene search engine replace need separate cluster
a: For ranked keyword search over your events, Centauri's native BM25 `SEARCH` (plus hybrid vector) often removes the need for a separate Elasticsearch cluster — and keeps it bi-temporal and zero-dependency. For massive document corpora a dedicated engine still wins.

q: Is it a blockchain or distributed ledger
t: blockchain ledger distributed crypto chain web3 dlt consensus
a: No. It has a tamper-evident SHA-256 hash chain so you can prove integrity, but it's a single-node database, not a distributed consensus ledger — auditability without the blockchain overhead.

q: Which AI agents or LLMs can use it
t: which agent llm claude gpt openai anthropic gemini model compatible support copilot
a: Any agent that speaks MCP (Claude and other MCP clients) or can make an HTTP call. No model lock-in — the model just needs to call a tool or a REST endpoint.

q: Is there support or a community
t: support community help enterprise commercial sla contact maintainer
a: It's open source — file issues and feature requests on GitHub (accepted features get implemented and shipped). Commercial/enterprise support is via Proxima360.

q: How can I contribute
t: contribute contributing pull request pr open source develop hack
a: PRs welcome on GitHub, and you can suggest a feature via the issue template — accepted ideas get implemented by the maintainers (sometimes by an AI).

q: What is on the roadmap
t: roadmap future encryption clustering plans coming soon next managed cloud
a: Encryption-at-rest with crypto-erasure, an ANN index for vectors at scale, and a fully managed Centauri Cloud. Single-node clustering/sharding is intentionally out of scope — partition by tenant instead.

q: What does the name Centauri mean
t: name meaning centauri proxima star why called branding
a: Centauri is the nearest star system to the Sun; the sponsor Proxima360 is named for Proxima Centauri, the nearest star — the nearest star to your data.

q: Who made Centauri
t: who made author company proxima360 centauri llc team built sponsor
a: Centauri is by Centauri LLC, sponsored by Proxima360 — the nearest star to your data. Source is on GitHub.

q: What is Centauri good for use cases and industries
t: use case examples industries good for fit retail healthcare finance security iot supply chain ml
a: Anywhere change, time, cause, and trust matter: retail price/promo integrity, financial audit and reconstruction, clinical decision replay, security incident lineage, ML feature provenance, compliance trails, and IoT/sensor drift.

q: How much does it cost to run
t: cost run hosting price expensive cheap resources server bill
a: The software is free. Running cost is just the box it runs on — sized to your RAM working set. One small VM (or the free/cheap tier on Render) is enough to start; there are no per-query or per-seat fees.

q: How do I connect Gemini GPT or another LLM to Centauri and hook it up
t: connect attach hook up wire integrate plug gemini gpt openai claude anthropic mistral llama llm agent function calling tool use example syntax how to setup
a: Any LLM with function/tool calling can use Centauri two ways. **HTTP:** declare one tool like `centauri_query(ceql)` whose handler does an HTTP `POST /v1/query` (body `{q: <your CeQL>}`, plus a bearer token) and let the model call it — the JSON response grounds its answer. **MCP:** for MCP-native clients, run `centauri mcp` (stdio) and point the client at it to get all 20 tools. Full Gemini + curl code is in the **For-agents** section of the site.

q: Show me example syntax or code to hook up an agent
t: example syntax code sample snippet python curl javascript wire integrate tool function call hook up rest api request gemini
a: Expose Centauri as one tool the model can call with a CeQL string. With curl: `curl -s localhost:7771/v1/query -H 'Authorization: Bearer TOKEN' -d '{q:"FACTS OF item:100001/store:4001 WHY"}'`. In Gemini/GPT, register a `centauri_query(ceql)` function whose body makes that POST and returns the response; the model picks when to call it. See the For-agents section for a runnable Gemini example.

q: How do I connect OpenAI ChatGPT or GPT-4
t: openai chatgpt gpt gpt4 gpt-4 gpt4o gpt-4o azure openai function calling tools assistant connect integrate api
a: Use OpenAI **function/tool calling**: define a `centauri_query(ceql)` function that POSTs to `/v1/query`, declare it in `tools=[...]`, and route the model's tool call to it. The exact same code works against any OpenAI-compatible endpoint — just change `base_url` and the model name. See example 7 in the For-agents section.

q: How do I connect Claude or Anthropic
t: claude anthropic tool use messages api mcp claude desktop claude code connect integrate
a: Easiest is **MCP** — Claude speaks it natively. Run `centauri mcp` and add it to your MCP client config (Claude Desktop/Code): mcpServers.centauri = command 'centauri', args ['mcp'] — zero code, all 20 tools. Or use Anthropic tool use via the Messages API with a `centauri_query` tool. See example 8.

q: How do I connect Grok or xAI
t: grok xai x.ai elon grok-2 grok2 connect integrate openai compatible
a: Grok (xAI) is **OpenAI-compatible** — use the OpenAI SDK with `base_url='https://api.x.ai/v1'` and the same `centauri_query` tool. The integration is identical to OpenAI; only the base URL and model name change. See example 9.

q: What about Mistral Cohere Llama Groq and other LLMs
t: mistral cohere llama llama3 together groq fireworks deepseek qwen openrouter perplexity openai compatible other models provider
a: Most providers (Mistral, Together, Groq, Fireworks, OpenRouter, DeepSeek, Qwen, Perplexity) expose an **OpenAI-compatible** API — point the OpenAI SDK at their `base_url` and register the same `centauri_query` tool. Cohere and others with native tool calling follow the same one-function pattern.

q: How do I use a local or self-hosted LLM Ollama LM Studio vLLM llama.cpp
t: local llm self hosted on prem on-premise private offline ollama lm studio lmstudio llama.cpp vllm gpt4all open source model air gapped no cloud
a: Local runners (**Ollama**, **LM Studio**, **vLLM**, llama.cpp) expose an OpenAI-compatible endpoint — point the OpenAI SDK at e.g. `http://localhost:11434/v1` (Ollama) and register the same `centauri_query` tool. Fully offline and private: the model, the tool, and Centauri all run on your own machine. See example 10.

q: Do I own my data and where does it live
t: own ownership data control where lives my data sovereignty self host on prem private device laptop server hostage
a: You do, completely. Your data lives wherever you point Centauri — your laptop, server, USB, NAS, a synced folder, or (roadmap) your own object-storage bucket — **never on our infrastructure**. It's one open file you can read, move, back up, or delete. No vendor can hold it hostage.

q: Do I pay license fees and how does cost compare to Oracle
t: license cost fee per core per seat oracle pricing tco expensive subscription support contract money
a: None. Centauri is **free and open source** (PostgreSQL-style license) — no per-core or per-seat licenses, no support contract. You pay only for hardware you already own. That's the core contrast with Oracle's licensing model: Oracle-grade memory for what happened, when, why, and how much to trust it, without the license.

q: Is there vendor lock-in
t: lock in lockin vendor proprietary open format export portable escape leave migrate
a: No. Open single-file format (JSONL), an open query language, and `centauri export -format csv` (or jsonl) any time. The binary has zero dependencies and runs anywhere — pick up your data and leave whenever you want.

q: Can I run it on my own cloud S3 Google Drive or device
t: own cloud s3 gcs azure bucket r2 google drive onedrive dropbox nas usb device self host where run storage backend infrastructure
a: Yes. Point Centauri at a local folder, USB, NAS, or a synced folder (OneDrive/Drive/Dropbox) today; a bring-your-own object-storage bucket (S3/GCS/Azure/R2) backend is on the roadmap. Your infrastructure, your control — see the Own-your-data design doc.
